title: stillingia trinervia
intro: Stillingia trinervia, also known as Queen's Delight or King's Cure, is a plant species native to the southeastern United States, belonging to the family Euphorbiaceae. The plant's roots and leaves have been traditionally used for their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which are attributed to the presence of saponins and other compounds. The health benefits of Stillingia trinervia include its use in treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its ability to support digestive health and boost the immune system. Stillingia trinervia can be prepared as a tincture, tea, or salve, with the roots typically being used for medicinal purposes, although it is essential to note that the plant should only be used under the guidance of a qualified healthcare professional.
